Chair for Neuroadaptive Technology, Artificial and Natural Intelligence Toulouse Institute (ANITI), France
Artificial and Natural Intelligence Toulouse Institute (ANITI)

title: CastillosBurstVEP100
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from 12 healthy participants performing a 4-class code-modulated Visual Evoked Potential (c-VEP) brain-computer interface task. The study compares novel burst c-VEP sequences with traditional m-sequences at two stimulus amplitude depths (100% and 40%) to optimize classification performance, minimize calibration requirements, and enhance user experience. Data were acquired at 500 Hz using a 32-channel BrainProducts system and processed with convolutional neural network architectures, achieving classification accuracies up to 95.6% with mean selection times of 1.5 seconds.
methods_description: EEG was recorded from 12 healthy participants (mean age 30.6±7.1 years) at 500 Hz using a 32-channel BrainProducts LiveAmp system with standard 10-20 montage and active electrodes. Participants performed an offline 4-class c-VEP task with factorial manipulation of pattern type (burst vs m-sequence) and amplitude depth (100% vs 40%). Visual stimuli were presented on a 60 Hz Dell monitor using PsychoPy software. Burst codes consisted of brief flashes (~50ms) with minimum 200ms inter-burst intervals, while m-sequences used Fibonacci-type LFSR with segmented 132-frame subsequences. Classification employed a CNN architecture with spatial (8×1, 16 filters), temporal (1×32, 8 filters), and 2D convolution (5×5, 4 filters) layers using 250ms sliding windows with 2ms stride and Pearson correlation for target selection.
